_meta.scale_caveat: VeSync BIA algorithm is calibrated for European/American body types. bf_pct may be understated or skewed — treat as directional trend only, NOT absolute truth. Use 4-week trend windows for bf_pct analysis. Weight (lb) and muscle_mass_lb are more reliable day-to-day signals. Visceral score is a useful relative marker.
_meta.note: BIA fluctuates with hydration/food timing; use trend direction not single readings; waist_cm=null until logged
_meta.fields: d=date; w=weight_lb; bf=body_fat_pct; mm=muscle_mass_lb; ffm=fat_free_mass_lb; skm=skeletal_muscle_pct; vis=visceral_fat; bmr=kcal; waist=waist_cm; hip=hip_cm; reading_time=HH:MM

readings.note: PM reading +0.8 lb; post-workout + evening food
up 2.5 lb; likely glycogen/sodium from leg day

stable vs prior day; last reading before vacation
